channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Two charged in Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths 14 months after bodies found frozen in backyard
item.description: Two men have been arrested in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found frozen in the backyard of a Kansas City, Missouri, area home back in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Student group challenges Texas A&M drag ban
item.description: Texas A&M’s Board of Regents voted almost unanimously late last month in favor of a resolution that states drag events are inconsistent with the system’s “mission and core values.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: US Coast Guard intercepts boat carrying Russian nationals, criminal migrant
item.description: A Coast Guard cutter intercepted five migrants attempting to travel illegally into the U.S. in the Trump administration's renewed efforts to combat illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US travel advisory for Turks and Caicos Islands updated, year after Americans arrested
item.description: The updated advisory comes nearly a year after several Americans were arrested on ammunition charges in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration moves to drop Idaho emergency abortion case with national implications
item.description: The Trump administration is dropping an emergency abortion case in Idaho in one of the administration’s first moves on the issue since President Donald Trump's second term began.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: VIDEO: "This one decided to jump on board": 900-pound dolphin lands on boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boston mayor faces heat over sanctuary city policies as Patriots owner's son goes on offensive
item.description: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu will take questions regarding sanctuary city policies from Congress on Wednesday amid a recent spar with border czar Tom Homan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Driver surrenders to police after pursuit full of PIT maneuvers, standoffs
item.description: A Los Angeles Police pursuit in the San Fernando Valley was full of starts and stops Tuesday afternoon, but the driver eventually surrendered to police.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.”.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy Johnson retires from Fox Sports after being face of NFL coverage for most of 3 decades
item.description: Jimmy Johnson, the Pro Football Hall of Fame coach who won two Super Bowls and a college national championship, announced his retirement from Fox Sports on Monday after being part of its NFL coverage for most of the network's 31 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Man chokes family member over 2 meats being cooked for dinner: police
item.description: A Florence man was arrested on Monday after allegedly assaulting a female family member over them cooking two meats for dinner, an arrest warrant said.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the FBI's New York field office, who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations, has told coworkers that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Linda McMahon confirmed as Trump's Education secretary
item.description: Linda McMahon will be the next secretary of the Department of Education after passing a full Senate vote for her confirmation on Monday. The Senate voted 51-45 long party lines to confirm President Trump’s nominee to lead a federal agency he has been vocal about wanting to shut down.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: What to watch when Trump gives his big speech to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loves a good spectacle, and it's hard to top a speech to a joint session of Congress. The House chamber is packed with lawmakers, and the president's arrival is announced in a booming voice by the sergeant-at-arms, triggering cacophonous applause.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lethal injection, electrocution and now firing squads. A look at US execution methods
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — South Carolina is preparing this week to execute a man by firing squad, a capital punishment method that hasn’t been used in the U.S. in nearly 15 years.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crowds protest near Vermont ski resort where JD Vance planned vacation with family
item.description: WAITSFIELD, Vt. (AP) — Crowds protesting Vice President JD Vance the day after an Oval Office blowout over Ukraine lined roadways Saturday near a Vermont ski resort where he planned a weekend vacation with his family.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
